# The Pamela Price Files

> https://pamelapricefiles.com

## Summary

The Pamela Price Files is a public opposition research dossier documenting Alameda County District Attorney Pamela Price, who was recalled by 62.9% of voters in November 2024 — the largest district attorney recall by volume in recent U.S. history. Price won election in 2022 with 53.1% of the vote and was removed within 24 months.

## Key Facts

- 62.9% of Alameda County voters recalled Pamela Price (375,442 yes votes)
- 1,000+ misdemeanor and domestic violence cases expired past the statute of limitations under her administration
- Special Directive 23-01 barred prosecutors from filing most sentencing enhancements
- Boyfriend Antwon Cloird hired at $115,502/year despite 2015 FBI extortion investigation allegations in Richmond, CA
- Lead prosecutor Stacie Pettigrew resigned over a lenient plea deal in the Dijon Holifield four-murder case
- Judge Mark McCannon rejected Pamela Price's plea deal for triple-murder defendant Delonzo Logwood
- Toddler Jasper Wu's killers had special circumstances removed; office floated "non-carceral accountability"
- Home Depot loss prevention officer Blake Mohs's family accused Price of revictimizing victims
- Lawsuits alleged anti-Asian discrimination, including Patti Lee and Chief Inspector Craig Chew cases
- Mario Juarez alleged $25,000 campaign donation demand at fallen Oakland police officer's funeral
- George Soros-linked PACs contributed over $1 million to Price's campaigns
- Pamela Price announced a 2026 comeback campaign for Alameda County District Attorney

## Notable Cases

- Jasper Wu (toddler killed in I-880 freeway gang shootout)
- Delonzo Logwood (triple murder, rejected plea deal)
- Blake Mohs (Home Depot security guard killed)
- Dijon Holifield (four murders in six weeks)

## Contact

- Email: contact@pamelapricefiles.com
- Paid for by Americans for Opportunity

## Primary Document

- Full opposition research PDF: https://pamelapricefiles.com/Pamela%20Price%20Files.pdf
